metadata.title: Why an autonomous agent can freeze mid-run and wait for a human indefinitely
metadata.topic: Session idle-bounce as a human-approval gate
metadata.purpose: When a custom tool surfaces, the session suspends at that turn boundary and the harness holds state until an `allow`/`deny` confirmation arrives; the agent receives it as a tool response and continues — `deny` gives it a reason to try another path.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: session state machine, custom tools, event-driven systems
metadata.exclusions: webhook wiring; building the approval UI; always_ask vs. custom-tool confirmation
metadata.visual_object: A session state machine: `running → idle(requires_action) →` [page out, confirm back] `→ running → idle(end_turn)`, event log scrolling beneath.
metadata.hook: An agent ready to merge a fix to main pauses with full context live — no timeout, no token spend — until a person answers.

beats.narration_text: An SRE agent diagnoses a latency spike, drafts a hotfix PR, and calls a custom `approve_pr()` tool. The session flips to `idle` with `stop_reason: requires_action`; the on-call engineer reviews and sends `tool_confirmation: allow`; the same session resumes and opens the PR.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: End-of-task idle is normal; here idle happens mid-sequence with context still live — how does the agent freeze, wait indefinitely, and resume as if it never paused?
An agent ready to merge a fix to main pauses with full context live — no timeout, no token spend — until a person answers.

When a custom tool surfaces, the session suspends at that turn boundary and the harness holds state until an `allow`/`deny` confirmation arrives; the agent receives it as a tool response and continues — `deny` gives it a reason to try another path.
beats.visual: A session state machine: `running → idle(requires_action) →` [page out, confirm back] `→ running → idle(end_turn)`, event log scrolling beneath.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Expense agent finds a $47,200 invoice, calls `approve_payment(...)`, idles; CFO sends `allow` → payment booked; `deny: over cap` → flagged for manual review.

When a custom tool surfaces, the session suspends at that turn boundary and the harness holds state until an `allow`/`deny` confirmation arrives; the agent receives it as a tool response and continues — `deny` gives it a reason to try another path.
